text:
traditional insurance & hedging is a $8T-$10T+ institutional dinosaur gatekept by suit-wearing middlemen 

prediction markets are the future of hedging, but right now they’re just a fractional drop in the bucket 

the edge? 

zero slow claims, pure binary contracts, and 6% costs vs 13% traditional over-the-counter fees

still low liquidity, but the retail to institutional pipeline is loading 

short the old guard, long polymarket & kalshi and upcoming infra on prediction markets

reality doesn’t have outcomes until they happen, only distributions of possibility

a continuous prediction market is the closest financial instrument to how the universe actually works before a result collapses into fact

the keynesian beauty contest from 1936 is still the most useful mental model for understanding prediction markets

keynes described a newspaper game where you don't win by picking the prettiest face. you win by picking the face you think everyone else will pick. the best players go deeper, picking the face they think everyone else thinks everyone else will pick

1/ first level is trading what you believe will happen 
2/ second level is trading what you think the crowd will believe
3/ third level and beyond is where the sharks live, trading what the crowd thinks the crowd will think and front-running sentiment before the wave forms

this is why prediction markets aren't simply truth machines and the price isn't a pure probability. 

it's a reflexive aggregation of what everyone thinks everyone thinks the probability is, but this doesn't apply to every market equally (it lives on a spectrum) 

"will it rain tomorrow" has almost no beauty contest dynamic because the weather doesn't care what the market thinks. here reflexivity has nowhere to enter so the price converges toward genuine probability 

"who wins a close election?" is the opposite, the odds get cited everywhere so the price gets contaminated by what people think the price means

the uncomfortable part is we can't cleanly separate genuine belief from reflexive positioning in real time. we can measure calibration after resolution but by then the composition that produced the price is gone

decomposing a prediction market price into real belief versus reflexive noise versus manipulation might be one of the most valuable unsolved problems in the space (if it's even a problem?) 

a big part of the thesis of prediction markets as truth machines depends on the answer and nobody has cracked it yet

the persistent rotation from nonproductive to productive crypto is the only trend that actually matters this cycle. 

prediction markets sit on the productive side and most people haven’t repriced that yet

the reflexivity problem nobody wants to discuss is that once a prediction market gets famous enough to be cited, it stops being a mirror and becomes an actor 

the odds influence the outcome they're trying to measure and polymarket's election odds moved sentiment which moved votes 

measurement changed the measured
RT @thenarrator: https://t.co/HDhYYxxZUk

i've spent the last year thinking and writing about prediction markets almost every day

talking to founders, connecting builders and sharing ideas that range from practical to probably delusional. most of it public, some of it in group chats and calls that nobody sees

the thing that keeps me going is a genuine belief that this primitive is underbuilt relative to its potential. prediction markets can reshape how the world prices uncertainty, allocates capital, and holds people accountable 

right now they're still treated as a betting vertical and that gap between what they are and what they could be is what drives me (and a lot of ppl)

the builders who are heads down right now on new mechanics, new primitives, and new products deserve more visibility and support than they're getting (we got some true all stars)

prediction markets deserve to be more than a sidebar during election season or sports games

the world cup will probably be one of the biggest events prediction markets have ever seen

kalshi showed what bracket challenges can do with their $1B march madness offer and now every platform is gearing up

one team worth watching is @gomarket: building for the global audience that most PM platforms aren't serving yet 

they're running a free knockout bracket challenge with a tesla on the line, no deposit just make your picks

pre-registration: https://t.co/jXXxIaksJN
https://t.co/HDhYYxxZUk
a very degen application of prediction markets: earn yield for the duration that you are right but lose it until you aren't

every analyst, politician, and news outlet is making predictions all day long but nobody keeps score. they just move on to the next take and hope you forgot the last one

someone should build a protocol that maps public claims against resolved prediction market outcomes and gives every public voice an accuracy 

the data from resolved markets already exists. nobody is using it to hold people accountable yet 

that feels like a big miss
i'm very optimistic about the future of prediction markets and onchain finance

prediction market UIs try to serve the beginner and the degen with the same screen

the thing is games solved this years ago

unlock the complexity as the user levels up, reveal leverage and the order book once they've shown they get the risk

harder to build, but the UI grows with the trader

from conversations this week i went in believing prediction markets could work as hedging tools and came out less sure than expected

the problem is simple: 

if your mortgage goes up by $200/month because of a rate hike, you need a hedge that pays you roughly $200 to offset it. a binary pm contract pays you everything or nothing

that's not a hedge, that's a bet that happens to be related to your problem

for pm hedging to become real, a few things need to be figured out:

> payout structures that scale proportionally with the outcome instead of all or nothing

> standardized contract specs where the relationship between the pm position and the real-world exposure is precise and auditable

> liquidity deep enough that meaningful hedge sizes don't move the market

> market designs where the instrument closely tracks the actual financial impact of the event, not just whether it happened

> risk frameworks that let lenders and insurers underwrite PM-based hedges the same way they underwrite traditional derivatives

none of this exists yet and doesn't feel like anyone is close but the demand side is real because the people who need simple affordable hedging tools vastly outnumber the people who can access traditional ones today

worth spending more time on (i have a long piece coming out on this soon)

the third prediction market incumbent won't be a startup. it'll be a market making firm or a brokerage like schwab

a lot of these provide the liquidity that makes kalshi and polymarket work and these firms have the matching engines, risk systems, regulatory licenses, and capital 

a brokerage or MM firm could launch a competitive PM exchange for a fraction of the cost and own most of the business by just deploying existing infrastructure into a new product category

the third seat will be taken by someone who already has every piece except the brand (for some)

was reading an interesting passage from a blog

the point: forecasting is almost never why a market exists. good prices are a side effect of money that was already moving for some other reason. find where the trading is already subsidized, and accuracy comes for free.

the subsidy is the product, not the question

been testing pm tools and strategies hands-on for a while and here’s what i’ve found actually works and what doesn’t (for me):

> most AI tools are worthless right now (even when given context). heard there are platforms leveraging profitable models but have not connected/seen any yet

> copy trading only works (barely) if you follow the right traders on the right markets (long-term markets seem to give you better chances)

> aggregators/terminals help but only if you already know what you’re doing (else you get lost in the sauce of too much info) but I’m expecting some aggregators to bridge that ux gap quickly 

> the pseudo quant strategy threads look impressive but the results don’t match

the real edge is embarrassingly simple: 

> be in group chats where actual traders share thoughts on specific markets and trade topics you genuinely understand or are willing to spend time learning

the person who deeply knows fed policy has more edge than any bot scanning headlines

one thing i didn’t expect: traders with perp market structure experience seem to adapt to PMs naturally. almost as if the skill transfers directly.

tldr: the edge if you’re new to this is not a tool, it’s context and being in rooms where real information flows

prop funds are turning prediction market trading into a funded career

skilled traders with edge but no capital can now access 5 figures+ capital and keep most of their profits

it’s the same model that built a multi-billion dollar industry in forex and futures. the people proving themselves on polymarket right now are building the track record that institutional PM desks will recruit from in very soon

I remember in 2023 jaredfromsubway was making ~$1M per day front running memecoin traders

was truly fascinating https://t.co/KL5q4ZIfrq

damn, it’s honestly unbelievable how many new prediction market projects and ideas have popped up recently

my weekend is basically booked. i need to go try all of them
